_comment: Per-model bone-role overrides for rig.js (tier 4), keyed by model URL (the same key as profiles.json). Most models need NO entry — the VRM/name/geometry tiers resolve them. Add one only when a specific rig is mis-identified. Shape per URL: { "roles": { "left_arm": "ExactBoneName", ... }  FORCES a role, beating vrm/name/geometry; "exclude": ["BoneName", ...]  blocks a bone from ever holding a role; "spring": { "extra": ["BoneName"], "never": ["BoneName"] }  force/suppress dangly-bone physics; "face": { "mouthMorph": <int> }  PINS the mouth-open morph INDEX (use only when a model's morphs are UNNAMED and the geometric jaw-drop detector picks wrong; must be a non-negative integer in range) }. A future mis-identified rig is a 1-line fix here — no code or regex changes.

./models/makiro/Makiro.glb._why: HairTOP is a SINGLE bone owning the whole upper hair mass — sprung alone it pivots as one rigid slab ('glitching, top half not flowing'; user 2026-06-10). It must ride the head rigidly; the BackStrand/FrontHair CHAINS keep their physics.

./models/lola_bunny/lola_bunny.glb._why: Her eyeball bones' parent frame flips the derived PITCH axis → eyes went UP when asked to look DOWN ('eyes go up instead of down', user 2026-06-11). Range also over-rotated the pupil out of the sclera into the head mesh ('go past her eye' / 'behind her skin'). Verified live via query eyegaze (down→fwd.y -0.359, up -0.466 = inverted). Reverse pitch + tight range. Tightened 0.40/0.22 → 0.24/0.14 because the pupil still clipped behind the skin at the extremes.
